entities.auspicioushelper/Anti0fZone.name: Anti 0f Zone
entities.auspicioushelper/Anti0fZone.description: Prevents certain zero-frameable inputs from happening while inside this entity

placements.holdables: Pick up holdables on the first possible pixel
placements.player_colliders: Hit player colliders on the first possible pixel (if speed or number of dashes changes, will end the frame's movement)
always_walljumpcheck: Check for walljumps off of non-solids each pixel (needed if using sideways jumpthrus) (slow; only use in the area directly around the jumpthru)
placements.triggers: Hit triggers on the first possible pixel
placements.solids: Perform walljumps/cornerjumps on first possible pixel (prevents missing cornerboost speed when buffering)
placements.cover_whole_room: Makes this entity cover the whole room

entities.auspicioushelper/ArbitraryDie.description: Kill area with the shape of a specified image

placements.image: Image to use as visuals and collider shape. Rooted in Graphics/Atlases/Gameplay/ (i.e. if using decal texture, decals/ must be added in front of the texture path used in the decal)
placements.CustomColliderPath: Override the above image for only the collider
placements.depth: Depth (negative is more in front; player is 0, fgtiles are -10000)
placements.alphaCutoff: Pixels whose alpha is higher than this value will be collidable

entities.auspicioushelper/ArbitrarySolid.description: Solid with the exact shape of a specified image. Suggested to use with pixel leniency trigger.

placements.safe: If berries can be collected while standing here
placements.soundIndex: Check wiki for a list of indices
placements.image: Image to use as visuals and collider shape. Rooted in Graphics/Atlases/Gameplay/ (i.e. if using decal texture, decals/ must be added in front of the texture path used in the decal)
placements.CustomColliderPath: Override the above image for only the collider
placements.depth: Depth (negative is more in front; player is 0, fgtiles are -10000)
placements.alphaCutoff: Pixels whose alpha is higher than this value will be collidable
placements.occludeLight: Whether this entitiy casts shadows (only the solid pixels). Relatively optimized; no need to worry about lag if there are not hundreds of these entities

entities.auspicioushelper/BeamBlocker.name: Beam Blocker
entities.auspicioushelper/BeamBlocker.description: Stops  Spiral Boss Beam from going through this entity, similar to a wall

entities.auspicioushelper/CampfireRespaw.description: Sets a respawn point on any safe ground if you hold duck long enough

placements.duckTime: How long you must duck to activate the campfire
placements.channel: Decrement this channel by 1 with each use. When it is 0, the campfire can no longer be used.
placements.disableNormal: Do not change your respawn when transitioning rooms

entities.auspicioushelper/CassetteFixerT.description: Fixes the bug where cassettes can appear one pixel higher/lower if dying when they are in the 'willchange' state

entities.auspicioushelper/SillySpikes.name: Relative Spikes
entities.auspicioushelper/SillySpikes.description: Spikes with recoloring options and options to fix common speed issues with spikes. With all options enabled, the spikes will kill you only if your velocity 'relative' to them is not moving away

placements.type: Texture to use. Rooted in Atlases/Gameplay/danger/spikes/
direction: the rotation value. Do not change this directly, use the R key on the selection. Changing in this box will break the entity.
placements.fancy: Fancy recoloring option. See wiki for details. For example (000,fff)->(fff,000)
placements.dreamThru: Lets you move through these spikes in a dream state (dreamblocks will go through these)
placements.useOwnSpeed: Whether to use this spike's own speed (from anything it may be attached to) when deciding whether to kill the player
placements.fixPickup: Stopping due to picking up a holdable while overlapping the spikes won't be deadly
placements.fixDash: Stopping due to starting a dash while overlapping the spikes won't be deadly
placements.fixOnBlock: Standing still on a block moving through these spikes won't be deadly if the block is moving in the right direction
placements.canAttach: Whether this entity attaches to solids

placements.texture: Textures to recolor. Can use fancy matching with *
placements.recolor: Fancy recoloring option. See wiki for details. For example (000,fff)->(fff,000)

entities.auspicioushelper/DieWater.name: Die Water
entities.auspicioushelper/DieWater.description: Water that uhm. uh. kills you

placements.resettable: Whether to unset the channel when you unenclose the horse

entities.auspicioushelper/MaterialApplie.description: For adding entities to shader layers

identifier: The entity ID path of the entity you want to include (found after ID: at the top of the loenn entity menu). Use 'player' for the player, 'fg' for foreground tiles and 'bg' for background tiles.
materialLayer: Identifier of the material layer to add the entity to
placements.dontNormalRender: Turn off normal rendering for this entity while in operation
placements.toggleChannel: Channel to toggle between modified/original rendering
placements.priority: The priority of this layer. Used when putting this entity in multiple layers simultaneously to decide which it appears in. (don't normal render will stop this item from rendering in lower priority layers as well as in normal usage)

entities.auspicioushelper/MaterialContro.name: Material Controller
entities.auspicioushelper/MaterialContro.description: Adds a shader pass to the level. Somewhat complicated; heavily encouraged to read materials section on wiki before usage

placements.passes: Effect passes used in the shader (consecutively)
placements.identifier: Shorthand for shader (only share identifiers for COMPLETELY IDENTICAL materials)
placements.params: Paramaters to pass to the shader
placements.textures: Textures to pass to the shader
placements.depth: The level at which the layer is added to the scene
placements.Fade_in: How to fade in during transitions
placements.fadeOut: How to fade out during transitions
placements.always: Whether to render even if the layer contains no entities (useful for styleground/fullscreen operations)
placements.drawInScene: Whether to render this layer (if unchecked, can still be chained in other layers)
placements.reload: Reload the effect whenever this entity is added (use for debugging)

entities.auspicioushelper/PortalGateH.name: Portal Gate (Horizontal)
entities.auspicioushelper/PortalGateH.description: A portal that slices entities that are halfway though it

right_facing_f0: If the first portal should face right
right_facing_f1: If the second portal should face right
placements.attached: If the portals are attached to solids
giveRCB: Whether the portal should give reverse corner boost on exiting portal
placements.instant_camera: Switch the camera when moving through the portals to keep the player at the same screen position

dont_unlock_bside: Whether to unlock the B-Side on collect
placements.use_raw_message: Whether to use the raw text in "message" or try and translate it
placements.message: The message to display on collect
placements.identifier: A unique identifier for this cassette
placements.flag: The flag to set on collect
placements.line_width: How wide the cassette collect message should be
